summary: PR #2613 split Kirby copied-special code out of `ftkirbyyoshiegg` into separate `ftkirbyspecialmars`, `ftkirbyspecialpeach`, and `ftkirbyspecialgamewatch` translation units, linked Peach and GameWatch as Matching objects, added headers/includes, and adjusted split/symbol metadata including local data labels.

key_files.role: split ranges for shortened `ftkirbyyoshiegg.c` and new Kirby special TUs; also adjusted an item data range
symbol metadata updates, including converting several address-style globals to local labels or local data symbols
build object registration: `ftkirbyspecialmars.c` as NonMatching, `ftkirbyspecialpeach.c` and `ftkirbyspecialgamewatch.c` as Matching
include surface updated from `ftkirbyyoshiegg.h` to the new split special headers
original monolithic source reduced after split-out
item source touched alongside Kirby/GameWatch split
item source touched alongside Kirby/Peach split

what_changed: `ftkirbyyoshiegg.c` was shortened from the broad text range `0x8010AA64..0x8010D740` to `0x8010AA64..0x8010B1F4`, with new split entries for `ftkirbyspecialmars.c`, `ftkirbyspecialpeach.c`, and `ftkirbyspecialgamewatch.c`.
`configure.py` added three Kirby special objects: Marth as NonMatching, Peach as Matching, and GameWatch as Matching.
`ftkirby.c` stopped including `ftkirbyyoshiegg.h` and added includes for the split Marth, Peach, and GameWatch headers.
New source/header pairs were added for Kirby copied specials: Marth, Peach, and GameWatch.
`symbols.txt` changed several data and sdata2 entries from global address-style symbols to local labels or local `@NNN` symbols around item, Kirby, and MSL math regions.

smart_moves.text: The PR paired source splitting with split-range metadata and configure object registration, so the new translation units are linked instead of only moving code in source.
The split kept the not-yet-matching Marth file explicitly marked NonMatching while marking Peach and GameWatch Matching in `configure.py`, avoiding a blanket status assumption for all split files.
Header include updates in `ftkirby.c` track the new per-special ownership rather than retaining the old `ftkirbyyoshiegg.h` include.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a monolithic recovered file is split into real translation units, update the split ranges, configure object list, and include surface in the same reviewable change.

Do not infer that all split-out files are matching just because some are linked as Matching; this PR explicitly leaves `ftkirbyspecialmars.c` NonMatching while linking Peach and GameWatch as Matching.

Split ownership can require symbol metadata cleanup from broad global address-style data symbols to local labels or local numbered data symbols, but such changes should be tied to split/symbol evidence rather than treated as a naming convention by itself.

naming_conventions.convention: New Kirby copied-special files follow `ftkirbyspecial<source_character>.c/.h` lowercase path naming, shown by `ftkirbyspecialmars`, `ftkirbyspecialpeach`, and `ftkirbyspecialgamewatch`.
GameWatch functions in the excerpt use the existing `ftKb_...Gw...` prefix style for public Kirby/GameWatch special routines, while unknown internal helpers remain address-style `fn_8010...` names.
Local data symbols in `symbols.txt` use numbered `@NNN` names when metadata marks them local, instead of keeping address-style global names.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use split text and small-data ranges to carve one old Kirby source range into separate translation units: YoshiEgg, Marth, Peach, and GameWatch.
Register each split file independently in `configure.py`, allowing mixed Matching and NonMatching status across split-out files.

The GameWatch source excerpt contains narrow `PAD_STACK` uses and inline helper extraction (`setGwVars`, `inline1`), but the PR slice does not include objdiff/build evidence explaining each stack tactic.
Symbol metadata was adjusted to local labels/local `@NNN` data symbols near affected data regions, consistent with split/data ownership work shown in `splits.txt` and `symbols.txt`.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching `src/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftkirbyspecialmars.c` to see when it moved from NonMatching to Matching.
Search for later cleanup of `PAD_STACK` in `ftkirbyspecialgamewatch.c` and whether objdiff evidence was recorded.
Search for follow-up symbol metadata changes around `0x804D9580..0x804D95B0` after the Kirby split.
Search for later edits to `itpeachtoad.c` and `itkirbygamewatchchefpan.c` associated with this split's local symbol changes.

notes: The PR identity, author, merge timestamp, changed-file list, and major split/configure/symbol changes are directly evidenced.
The raw diff is truncated in the loaded file, so source-level details for Peach, Marth, headers, and item edits are only partially visible through metadata and excerpts.
There are no loaded review comments or reviews; `review_feedback` is therefore empty.
No build logs, objdiff reports, or regression outputs are loaded; Matching/NonMatching status is reported only as configured in `configure.py`, not independently verified.
Observed `PAD_STACK` and inline-helper tactics in the GameWatch excerpt are not promoted as globally accepted tactics because the slice lacks verifier rationale.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Split/link PRs should update source ownership, split metadata, configure objects, and includes together
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2613 demonstrates a coherent split/link pattern: carve new text/sdata/sdata2 ranges in `splits.txt`, add new source/header pairs, register each object in `configure.py`, and update dependents such as `ftkirby.c` includes.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Mixed Matching status is valid after splitting a monolithic file
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2613, `ftkirbyspecialpeach.c` and `ftkirbyspecialgamewatch.c` were added as Matching objects while `ftkirbyspecialmars.c` was added as NonMatching, so status should be tracked per split file.

source_update_candidates.title: PR #2613 split Kirby YoshiEgg range into Marth, Peach, and GameWatch special files
source_update_candidates.text: `ftkirbyyoshiegg.c` was reduced to text `0x8010AA64..0x8010B1F4`; new split files were added for `ftkirbyspecialmars.c` (`0x8010B1F4..0x8010C288`), `ftkirbyspecialpeach.c` (`0x8010C288..0x8010CE5C`), and `ftkirbyspecialgamewatch.c` (`0x8010CE5C..0x8010D740`).
source_update_candidates.reason: This is concrete path/split ownership metadata from the PR diff and may be useful for path-fact curation.

source_update_candidates.title: Configure status for split Kirby special files in PR #2613
source_update_candidates.reason: Useful as a historical path/status fact, but should remain a source-owned fact rather than an independently validated match claim.

source_update_candidates.title: Treat stack-padding tactics in split-out files as evidence-requiring
source_update_candidates.text: The GameWatch split excerpt contains `PAD_STACK` uses while the loaded PR slice has no verifier rationale. This supports reinforcing the existing standard that stack-padding tactics need explicit objdiff/build evidence, not creating a new accepted exception.
source_update_candidates.reason: Candidate note for standards curation: this PR is an example of why PAD_STACK observations should be annotated with verification evidence before being treated as reviewable style.

rejection_notes: Do not promote the PR's merged state or configure `Object(Matching, ...)` entries as independent objdiff validation; no build/objdiff logs are loaded.
Do not infer reviewer intent or accepted review rationale; there are no loaded reviews or review comments.
Do not generalize the observed `PAD_STACK` uses in `ftkirbyspecialgamewatch.c` into a preferred style; evidence only shows their presence.
Do not treat all symbol global-to-local changes as a universal naming rule; the evidence is specific to this split/symbol metadata diff.
